source.title: Cortical substrates for exploratory decisions in humans

confidence_rationale: Anchor for MECH-314b (frontopolar uncertainty-driven curiosity) as a substrate distinct from MECH-314a (striatal novelty, Wittmann 2007) and MECH-314c (learning progress, Kaplan & Oudeyer 2007). Daw 2006 isolates frontopolar cortex as the locus of exploratory decisions in a four-armed bandit — a decision driven by Kalman-filter uncertainty over each arm's payoff. The substrate is anatomically distinct from the SN/VTA novelty locus and from the cortical microcircuit that Kaplan & Oudeyer propose for learning-progress signalling.
failure_signatures: Daw 2006 collapses all exploration into a single binary classification (exploratory vs exploitative) on each trial. Within-exploration sub-types (novelty-driven, uncertainty-driven, learning-progress-driven) are not separately measured. The fact that frontopolar BOLD tracks exploration in a Kalman-uncertainty paradigm does not establish that frontopolar is exclusively the substrate for uncertainty-driven exploration as opposed to other curiosity flavours.

The paper uses a stationary-bandit task; novelty is not manipulated independently of uncertainty (every arm is uncertain in proportion to how few times it has been sampled, so novelty and uncertainty are confounded by design).

mapping.source_claim_statement: Human exploratory decisions in a four-armed bandit are preferentially associated with BOLD activity in frontopolar cortex and intraparietal sulcus, dissociable from striatum/vmPFC activity during exploitative decisions. Exploration is computationally driven by a softmax policy with Kalman-filter uncertainty over arm payoffs.
mapping.ree_translation: Daw 2006 anchors MECH-314b's substrate identity: the frontopolar locus of uncertainty-driven curiosity. Combined with Wittmann 2007 (MECH-314a = SN/VTA-striatal novelty) and Kaplan & Oudeyer 2007 (MECH-314c = cortical microcircuit learning-progress signal via tonic DA), this gives Q-044 three distinct substrate loci to test. For Q-044 the three-arm ablation prediction is that knocking out MECH-314b should produce a frontopolar-loss signature (worse uncertainty-driven choice) distinct from the striatal-loss signature of MECH-314a-OFF and the slower learning-progress decay signature of MECH-314c-OFF.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Daw 2006's task confounds novelty with uncertainty by design (a less-sampled arm is both newer and more uncertain). The frontopolar BOLD effect could be partly driven by novelty rather than purely by uncertainty, leaving room for ambiguity in the 314a-vs-314b distinction. The Wittmann 2007 SN/VTA novelty paradigm pulls these apart (cued familiar vs novel) and is the cleaner anchor for novelty specifically; Daw 2006 is the cleaner anchor for the frontopolar locus.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI, n=14, four-armed bandit with drifting payoffs; healthy young adults. (Reused from Q-043 lit-pull — same primary evidence, different theoretical role.)

confidence_components.notes: Same source quality as the Q-043 entry. Mapping fidelity is lower here (0.65 vs 0.55) because the within-curiosity distinction Q-044 asks about is partly conflated by Daw's task design. The substrate locus identification (frontopolar) is strong; the substrate-content identification (uncertainty vs novelty) is weaker. The 0.78 aggregate keeps the contribution to lit_conf modest given the confound.
